name: Release desktop

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'desktop-v*'

concurrency:
  group: desktop-release-${{ github.ref }}

jobs:
  prepare:
    name: Prepare draft release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
      tag: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.tag }}
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - name: Resolve version
        id: ver
        run: |
          tag="${{ github.ref_name }}"
          echo "tag=$tag" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=${tag#desktop-v}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Create draft release if missing
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          if gh release view "${{ steps.ver.outputs.tag }}" --repo "${{ github.repository }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Release already exists, reusing."
          else
            gh release create "${{ steps.ver.outputs.tag }}" \
              --repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
              --draft \
              --title "Lightfast desktop v${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}" \
              --notes "Automated release of @lightfast/desktop."
          fi

  build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.arch }}
    needs: prepare
    runs-on: macos-14
    timeout-minutes: 60
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        arch: [arm64, x64]
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
      GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY: ${{ secrets.APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY }}
      APPLE_TEAM_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_TEAM_ID }}
      APPLE_API_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_KEY_ID }}
      APPLE_API_ISSUER: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_ISSUER }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js 22
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Stamp version + feed URL in package.json
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        run: |
          npm version "${{ needs.prepare.outputs.version }}" --no-git-tag-version --allow-same-version
          npm pkg set buildFlavor=prod
          npm pkg set buildNumber="$GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER"
          npm pkg set sparkleFeedUrl='https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/latest/download/latest-mac-${arch}.json'

      - name: Import Apple signing certificate
        env:
          APPLE_CERT_BASE64: ${{ secrets.APPLE_CERT_BASE64 }}
          APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD }}
          KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          echo "$APPLE_CERT_BASE64" | base64 -d > /tmp/cert.p12
          security create-keychain -p "$KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD" build.keychain
          security default-keychain -s build.keychain
          security unlock-keychain -p "$KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD" build.keychain
          security set-keychain-settings -t 3600 -u build.keychain
          security import /tmp/cert.p12 -k build.keychain -P "$APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD" -T /usr/bin/codesign
          security set-key-partition-list -S apple-tool:,apple:,codesign: -s -k "$KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD" build.keychain
          rm /tmp/cert.p12

      - name: Write notarize API key
        env:
          APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT }}
          APPLE_API_KEY_ID_SECRET: ${{ secrets.APPLE_API_KEY_ID }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p "$HOME/.private_keys"
          key_file="$HOME/.private_keys/AuthKey_${APPLE_API_KEY_ID_SECRET}.p8"
          printf '%s' "$APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT" > "$key_file"
          echo "APPLE_API_KEY=$key_file" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Publish (forge)
        working-directory: apps/desktop
        run: pnpm exec electron-forge publish --arch=${{ matrix.arch }} --platform=darwin

  finalize:
    name: Finalize release
    needs: [prepare, build]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Generate Squirrel.Mac update feed
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GH_REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.tag }}
          RELEASE_VERSION: ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.version }}
        run: node apps/desktop/scripts/generate-update-feed.mjs

      - name: Publish release (undraft)
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          gh release edit "${{ needs.prepare.outputs.tag }}" \
            --repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
            --draft=false

---
# Verify that changesets follow semantic versioning guidelines
# - patch: Bug fixes, performance improvements  
# - minor: New features, non-breaking changes
# - major: Breaking changes, API changes
name: Verify Changesets

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled, unlabeled]
    branches:
      - main
    paths:
      - '.changeset/*.md'

jobs:
  verify-changesets:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Get changed changeset files
        id: changeset-files
        run: |
          echo "changed-files=$(git diff --diff-filter=dr --name-only $BASE_SHA -- '.changeset/*.md' | tr '\n' ' ')" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        env:
          BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}

      - name: Verify changeset format
        if: steps.changeset-files.outputs.changed-files != ''
        run: |
          echo "Checking changeset files: ${{ steps.changeset-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
          
          for file in ${{ steps.changeset-files.outputs.changed-files }}; do
            echo "Checking $file..."
            
            # Check if file exists
            if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
              echo "::error::Changeset file $file not found"
              exit 1
            fi
            
            # Check if it mentions lightfast or @lightfastai/mcp
            if ! grep -qE "(\"lightfast\"|\"@lightfastai/mcp\"|\"@lightfastai/cli\")" "$file"; then
              echo "::error::Changeset $file must include lightfast, @lightfastai/mcp, or @lightfastai/cli package"
              exit 1
            fi

            # Check for valid version types
            if ! grep -E "^\"(lightfast|@lightfastai/mcp|@lightfastai/cli)\": (patch|minor|major)$" "$file"; then
              echo "::error::Changeset $file must use patch, minor, or major for lightfast, @lightfastai/mcp, or @lightfastai/cli"
              exit 1
            fi
            
            # Check for summary
            if ! grep -A 5 "^---$" "$file" | tail -n +2 | grep -v "^---$" | grep -q "."; then
              echo "::error::Changeset $file must include a summary description"
              exit 1
            fi
            
            echo "✓ $file is valid"
          done
          
          echo "All changesets are valid!"

name: Core CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

# Cancel previous runs on new pushes to the same PR
concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  quality:
    name: Quality
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }}
      TURBO_TEAM: ${{ vars.TURBO_TEAM }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js 22
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Lint (biome)
        run: pnpm check

      - name: Type check packages
        env:
          SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION: "true"
        run: pnpm turbo typecheck --filter=lightfast --filter=@lightfastai/mcp --filter=@lightfastai/cli --continue

  # Test (simple test run on Node 22)
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }}
      TURBO_TEAM: ${{ vars.TURBO_TEAM }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js 22
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Test lightfast
        env:
          SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION: "true"
        run: pnpm --filter lightfast test

  # Build check (runs on Node 22)
  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }}
      TURBO_TEAM: ${{ vars.TURBO_TEAM }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js 22
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build packages
        env:
          SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION: "true"
        run: pnpm turbo build --filter=lightfast --filter=@lightfastai/mcp --filter=@lightfastai/cli

      - name: Check build outputs
        run: |
          echo "lightfast build outputs:"
          ls -la core/lightfast/dist/
          echo ""
          echo "@lightfastai/mcp build outputs:"
          ls -la core/mcp/dist/
          echo ""
          echo "@lightfastai/cli build outputs:"
          ls -la core/cli/dist/
          echo "All builds completed successfully"

  # Summary job (required status check)
  core-ci-success:
    name: Core CI Success
    if: always()
    needs: [quality, test, build]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Evaluate results
        run: |
          quality="${{ needs.quality.result }}"
          test="${{ needs.test.result }}"
          build="${{ needs.build.result }}"

          echo "quality.result   = $quality"
          echo "test.result      = $test"
          echo "build.result     = $build"

          if [[ "$quality" != "success" || \
                "$test" != "success" || \
                "$build" != "success" ]]; then
            echo "One or more Core CI jobs failed."
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "All Core CI checks passed."

content.text:
I have enough context. Here's my read on current state and a proposed next batch.

## What's Already Done (since the research was written)

All four quick-win phases from `2026-04-23-desktop-codex-gap-quick-wins.md` have been applied:

- **Phase 1 — entitlements diet**: `build/entitlements.mac.plist` now has only 5 keys (no `disable-library-validation`/`device.camera`/`network.server`). ✅
- **Phase 2 — Info.plist hygiene**: `forge.config.ts:67-78` has `NSQuitAlwaysKeepsWindows=false`, `LSMinimumSystemVersion=12.0`, `LSEnvironment.MallocNanoZone=0`. ✅
- **Phase 3 — Sentry tags + RewriteFrames**: `src/main/sentry.ts:14-55` has `SESSION_ID`, `rewriteFramesIntegration({root, prefix: "app:///"})`, `dist=buildNumber`, and `bundle/host/sessionId` tags. ✅
- **Phase 4 — dead code**: `showContextMenu` gone from `ipc.ts`, `silentRefresh`/`protocol.ts` deleted, OAuth was reworked to a cleaner **loopback HTTP server** flow (`auth-flow.ts:70-141`) that obsoletes the deep-link dispatcher entirely. ✅

## What Still Blocks Production Release

### Real code/CI gaps (things I can plan)

1. **Sentry source-map upload in CI** — `rewriteFramesIntegration` is already wired with `app:///` prefix but no CI step uploads the maps. First crash report from a packaged build will be minified garbage. `@sentry/cli` is in `pnpm-workspace.yaml:82` `onlyBuiltDependencies` (installed, never invoked).
2. **`desktop-release.yml.disabled` → enabled** — needs rename + these hardening edits: no `cancel-in-progress`, no Sentry release step, no source-map upload, no artifact attestation, static "Automated release" notes.
3. **CI coverage for desktop** — `ci-core.yml:45,105` **explicitly excludes** desktop from typecheck + build. `ci.yml` only runs desktop typecheck via `turbo --affected`. Nothing builds/packages desktop on every PR — packaging regressions reach `main`.
4. **`verify-changeset.yml:51`** — allowlist blocks a `@lightfast/desktop` changeset. Need a decision: allow changesets, or commit to tag-only (`desktop-v*`) releases.
5. **Release host repo** — `forge.config.ts:43-54` reads `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` (dedicated repo) but `desktop-release.yml.disabled:54` passes `${{ github.repository }}` (monorepo). The two are in conflict; first release will fail-closed until resolved.
6. **Build-metadata stamping in the workflow** — the workflow stamps `version`/`buildFlavor`/`buildNumber`/`sparkleFeedUrl` but misses `sparklePublicKey` (stays `""`, which is fine since we're on Electron `autoUpdater` not Sparkle-native — but worth a comment or removing the field entirely to avoid confusion).
7. **`.env.example` for desktop** — undocumented env surface (`LIGHTFAST_API_URL`, `SENTRY_DSN`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY`, `SPARKLE_FEED_URL`, `BUILD_FLAVOR`).

### External setup you need to do (not code)

**Apple Developer** — 9 things:
- Apple Developer Program enrollment ($99/yr)
- **Developer ID Application** certificate → export `.p12` with password
- App Store Connect API key → `.p8` file + key ID + issuer UUID
- Add these 8 secrets to the GitHub repo hosting the workflow:
  - `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY` (e.g. `Developer ID Application: Lightfast AI Inc (TEAMID)`)
  - `APPLE_TEAM_ID` (10-char)
  - `APPLE_CERT_BASE64` (`base64 < cert.p12`)
  - `APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD` (the p12 password)
  - `APPLE_API_KEY_ID` (10-char)
  - `APPLE_API_ISSUER` (UUID)
  - `APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT` (contents of `AuthKey_XXX.p8`)
  - `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD` (any strong random string; CI-local only)

**Sentry** — 4 things:
- Create `lightfast-desktop` project in your Sentry org
- Grab the DSN → stored as `SENTRY_DSN` secret (CI) + shipped via env at runtime
- Create a Sentry auth token with `project:releases` scope → `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` secret
- Note `SENTRY_ORG` and `SENTRY_PROJECT` values for the CLI commands

**GitHub release repo decision** — pick one:
- **(a)** Ship from the monorepo itself (simpler, tag `desktop-v*` on main). Requires dropping `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` logic in `forge.config.ts`.
- **(b)** Dedicated public repo `lightfastai/lightfast-desktop-releases` (cleaner public artifact URLs, no need to make monorepo releases public). Requires a cross-repo PAT for the workflow.

## Proposed Next Batch

**In scope (pre-release, ~1–2 days code):**

1. **Phase A — Release-repo resolution** (tiny, unblocks everything)
2. **Phase B — Source-map upload to Sentry** (new script in `apps/desktop/scripts/`, Sentry release step in workflow)
3. **Phase C — Harden & enable `desktop-release.yml`** (rename off `.disabled`, add cancel-in-progress, attestation, real release notes, Sentry release step, fix `sparklePublicKey` placeholder)
4. **Phase D — Desktop CI coverage** (add desktop to `ci-core.yml` typecheck, add `ci.yml` job that runs `electron-forge package` on macos for PRs touching desktop)
5. **Phase E — Contributor ergonomics** (`.env.example`, `lint` script, fix or rename `dev:desktop-stack`, changeset allowlist decision)

**Explicitly deferred (not blocking v0.1.0):**

- File-backed main-process logger (big lift; Sentry breadcrumbs cover most of the need if properly wired)
- Sparkle-native Ed25519 auto-update (Squirrel.Mac is fine for v0.1.0)
- Windows/Linux makers, universal binary, SQLite, workers, i18n, Playwright harness

## Questions Before I Write the Plan

1. **Release repo**: (a) monorepo tags, or (b) dedicated `lightfast-desktop-releases` repo? This is the highest-leverage decision — affects download URLs, workflow auth, and public-vs-private of the monorepo.
2. **First release arch**: arm64-only (matches Codex, ~half the CI time), or both arm64+x64 from day one (what the disabled workflow already does)?
3. **Sentry DSN delivery**: bake into `package.json`/`build-info.ts` at CI time (simplest, DSN is public anyway), or keep injecting via `SENTRY_DSN` env at launch (requires an entry in the `.app` bundle's env somehow — hard on macOS; usually means a wrapper)?
4. **Changeset policy for desktop**: allowlist `@lightfast/desktop` in `verify-changeset.yml` so it participates in the changeset flow, or commit to **tag-only releases** for desktop (no changeset entries needed)?
5. **File logger**: in-scope for first release or genuinely punt? My read: **punt** — Sentry + breadcrumbs is enough for v0.1.0, and adding a file logger is a full sub-phase by itself. Confirm?

Once you answer 1–5 I'll write the detailed plan to `thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-23-desktop-pre-release-batch.md`.

content.text:
## Recommendation: **(a) monorepo tags**

Monorepo is public (`lightfastai/lightfast`, confirmed via `gh repo view`) — that removes the only real reason to split: Sparkle feed URLs and download links work without auth for end users.

**Why (a) over (b):**

- **Zero extra auth plumbing.** The workflow's `GITHUB_TOKEN` already has `contents:write` on this repo. A dedicated repo needs a fine-grained PAT stored as a new secret, which is one more thing that can expire/rotate.
- **Source + tag + artifact live together.** `desktop-v0.1.0` tag points directly at the commit it was built from. No cross-repo commit hash dance when debugging "which commit shipped in 0.1.0."
- **No namespace collision with `release.yml`.** Changesets produces `lightfast@x.y.z` / `@lightfastai/mcp@x.y.z` tags; desktop would use `desktop-v*`. Different prefixes, one release page — users actually benefit from seeing everything in one place.
- **Artifact size isn't a problem.** GitHub release asset storage is unmetered. ~200MB per release (arm64+x64 zip+dmg) is fine.

**Concrete edits this implies for the plan:**

- `desktop-release.yml.disabled:54` already uses `${{ github.repository }}` — keep as-is.
- `apps/desktop/forge.config.ts:43-54` reads `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` — **remove that indirection**, hardcode `{ owner: "lightfastai", name: "lightfast" }` in the `PublisherGithub` config. One less env var, one less way to misconfigure.
- Sparkle feed URL becomes the stable `https://github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/releases/latest/download/latest-mac-${arch}.json` — which is what the workflow already stamps.

**The one tradeoff you're accepting:** monorepo release page gets desktop binaries alongside npm release entries. I think that's a feature, not a bug — it's the honest shape of what you ship.

Ready for your call on 2–5 and I'll write the plan.

content.text:
## Recommendation: `@lightfast/desktop@<version>`

Matches the exact `name` in `apps/desktop/package.json:2` and the changesets convention already used in this repo (`release.yml` produces `lightfast@x.y.z` and `@lightfastai/mcp@x.y.z` tags). One tagging rule across the whole monorepo beats two.

**The landscape:**

| Convention | Example | Used by |
|---|---|---|
| **`<package>@<version>`** | `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0` | Changesets, Lerna, Nx, Turborepo, Tauri, Expo — the de facto standard for pnpm/npm monorepos |
| `<product>/v<version>` | `desktop/v0.1.0` | Some ad-hoc monorepos (git treats `/` as namespace) |
| `<shortname>-v<version>` | `desktop-v0.1.0` | Older setups — what the disabled workflow currently has |
| `v<version>` | `v0.1.0` | Single-product repos (VS Code, Signal, Obsidian, Electron itself) — **not appropriate in a monorepo** |

**Why `@lightfast/desktop@<version>` wins here specifically:**

1. **Consistency** — your repo already tags npm packages this way via `changesets/action`. Users scanning `git tag -l` see one pattern.
2. **Future-proof for changesets** — if you ever allowlist `@lightfast/desktop` in `verify-changeset.yml` (my Q4), changesets will produce this exact tag format. Pre-adopting it means no migration later.
3. **Self-documenting** — the tag IS the package identifier. You can paste it anywhere and it's unambiguous which artifact it points to.

**Concrete edits for the plan:**

- `desktop-release.yml.disabled:6` — change trigger from `desktop-v*` to `'@lightfast/desktop@*'` (the quotes matter in YAML because of the `@`).
- `desktop-release.yml.disabled:23-26` — change the `version` extraction from `${tag#desktop-v}` to `${tag#@lightfast/desktop@}`.
- Document the convention in `apps/desktop/README.md` so contributors know how to cut a release (`git tag @lightfast/desktop@0.1.0 && git push origin @lightfast/desktop@0.1.0`).

**The one caveat:** `@` needs shell-quoting in some contexts (`git push origin "@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0"`). Tolerable — it's the same caveat that already applies to `@lightfastai/mcp@*` tags. If you ever find it annoying, `desktop@<version>` (no scope) is the fallback that keeps changesets-compat but avoids the leading `@`. But I wouldn't preemptively pick that — stay consistent with the existing tags.

Ready for 2–5.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

# Cancel previous runs on new pushes to the same PR
concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  quality:
    name: Quality
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }}
      TURBO_TEAM: ${{ vars.TURBO_TEAM }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js 22
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Check OpenAPI spec is up to date
        run: |
          pnpm turbo generate:openapi
          git diff --exit-code packages/app-api-contract/openapi.json || {
            echo "::error::openapi.json is stale. Run 'pnpm turbo generate:openapi' and commit the result."
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Lint (biome)
        run: pnpm check

      - name: Type check affected packages
        env:
          SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION: "true"
          TURBO_SCM_BASE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.event.before }}
        run: pnpm turbo typecheck --affected --continue

      - name: Check boundaries
        run: pnpm turbo boundaries

      - name: Check dead code and unused dependencies
        continue-on-error: true # Remove after initial cleanup sprint
        run: pnpm knip

  ci-success:
    name: CI Success
    if: always()
    needs: [quality]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Evaluate results
        run: |
          quality="${{ needs.quality.result }}"

          echo "quality.result = $quality"

          if [[ "$quality" != "success" ]]; then
            echo "CI quality checks failed."
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "All CI checks passed."

name: Release lightfast

on:
  push:
    paths:
      - '.changeset/**'
      - '.github/workflows/release.yml'
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}

jobs:
  release:
    name: Release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - name: Configure git user
        run: |
          git config --global user.name 'lightfast-release-bot'
          git config --global user.email 'releases@lightfast.ai'

      - name: Checkout Repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.LIGHTFAST_RELEASE_BOT_GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js 22
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build packages
        env:
          SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION: "true"
        run: pnpm turbo build --filter lightfast --filter @lightfastai/mcp --filter @lightfastai/cli

      - name: Run tests
        env:
          SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION: "true"
        run: pnpm --filter lightfast test

      - name: Dry-run publish to validate packages
        run: |
          echo "Running publish dry-run to validate both packages..."
          pnpm changeset publish --dry-run
          echo "✅ Dry-run successful - both packages are valid"

      - name: Create Release Pull Request or Publish to npm
        id: changesets
        uses: changesets/action@v1
        with:
          version: pnpm changeset version
          publish: pnpm changeset publish
          setupGitUser: false
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.LIGHTFAST_RELEASE_BOT_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.LIGHTFAST_RELEASE_BOT_NPM_TOKEN }}
          NPM_CONFIG_PROVENANCE: true

      - name: Verify published versions match
        if: steps.changesets.outputs.published == 'true'
        run: |
          echo "Verifying both packages published successfully..."
          sleep 10  # Wait for npm registry to update

          LIGHTFAST_VERSION=$(npm view lightfast version)
          MCP_VERSION=$(npm view @lightfastai/mcp version)

          CLI_VERSION=$(npm view @lightfastai/cli version 2>/dev/null || echo "not-published")

          echo "lightfast: $LIGHTFAST_VERSION"
          echo "@lightfastai/mcp: $MCP_VERSION"
          echo "@lightfastai/cli: $CLI_VERSION"

          if [ "$LIGHTFAST_VERSION" != "$MCP_VERSION" ]; then
            echo "❌ ERROR: Version drift detected!"
            echo "lightfast@$LIGHTFAST_VERSION != @lightfastai/mcp@$MCP_VERSION"
            echo "Manual intervention required - publish the missing package"
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "✅ Both packages at $LIGHTFAST_VERSION"

      - name: Send a Slack notification if a publish happens
        if: steps.changesets.outputs.published == 'true'
        # TODO: First users are through Slack/Discord channels. Notify via Pylon + Lightfast
        # integration to post release notes to each team's custom channel.
        run: echo "A new version of lightfast was published!"

---
date: 2026-04-23
owner: jp@jeevanpillay.com
branch: main
based_on: thoughts/shared/research/2026-04-23-codex-vs-lightfast-desktop-production-gap.md
supersedes_partial: thoughts/shared/plans/2026-04-23-desktop-codex-gap-quick-wins.md
status: draft
---

# Desktop Pre-Release Batch Implementation Plan

## Overview

Get `apps/desktop` from "quick-wins applied" to "first public `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0` release shipping a signed, notarized, auto-updating macOS binary with Sentry-readable stack traces." Six phases. Phase A is a human-setup checklist; B–F are code/CI changes. All four prior quick-win phases are already applied and verified in the current tree.

## Current State Analysis

All four phases of `2026-04-23-desktop-codex-gap-quick-wins.md` are landed:
- Entitlements trimmed (`build/entitlements.mac.plist` has 5 keys; over-broad `disable-library-validation`/`device.camera`/`network.server` removed).
- `forge.config.ts:67-78` has `NSQuitAlwaysKeepsWindows=false`, `LSMinimumSystemVersion=12.0`, `LSEnvironment.MallocNanoZone="0"`.
- `src/main/sentry.ts:14-55` has `SESSION_ID`, `rewriteFramesIntegration({root, prefix: "app:///"})`, `dist=buildNumber`, `bundle/host/sessionId` tags.
- `showContextMenu` IPC, `silentRefresh`, and the deep-link `console.log` are gone. `protocol.ts` deleted; OAuth moved to a loopback HTTP server (`auth-flow.ts:70-141`).

What still blocks a v0.1.0 release:
- `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml.disabled` is inert (correctly, since Apple signing secrets aren't provisioned yet).
- `forge.config.ts:43-54` reads `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` but the disabled workflow passes `${{ github.repository }}` — two conflicting paths.
- `@sentry/cli` is in `pnpm-workspace.yaml:82` `onlyBuiltDependencies` but no script invokes it; packaged-build stack traces will be unreadable despite `rewriteFramesIntegration` being wired.
- `ci-core.yml:45,105` explicitly excludes desktop from typecheck + build. `ci.yml:50-54` only runs typecheck via `turbo --affected`. Nothing runs `electron-forge package` on PRs.
- `apps/desktop/package.json` placeholders: `version: "0.0.0"`, empty `sparkleFeedUrl`, unused `sparklePublicKey: ""`.
- No `.env.example`, no `lint`/`clean` scripts in desktop, `dev:desktop-stack` is misnamed at the root.

### Key Discoveries

- `sparklePublicKey` is declared in `src/shared/env.ts:12`, `src/shared/ipc.ts:52`, and `src/main/build-info.ts:23` but has **zero** downstream readers (grep confirms). Electron's `autoUpdater` does not use it. Drop the field instead of stamping it in CI.
- Sentry DSNs are public identifiers (they're visible in any shipped app's JS bundle). Baking at CI build time via `npm pkg set sentryDsn=...` from a secret is simplest — no runtime env plumbing required on macOS.
- Release tag convention in this repo is already `<package>@<version>` (changesets produces `lightfast@x.y.z`, `@lightfastai/mcp@x.y.z` via `release.yml`). Desktop should match: `@lightfast/desktop@<version>`. YAML trigger: `'@lightfast/desktop@*'`.
- Desktop is `"private": true`; `changesets/action` ignores private packages automatically — it cannot and will not try to publish desktop. The blocker isn't publishing, it's that `verify-changeset.yml:51` rejects any changeset file mentioning `@lightfast/desktop`. We stay tag-only and add the package to `.changeset/config.json` `ignore` so `pnpm changeset` never prompts for it.
- Monorepo is public (`gh repo view` → `"visibility":"PUBLIC"`). Sparkle feed URL `github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/releases/latest/download/latest-mac-${arch}.json` works without auth.
- `.vite/build` (main + preload) and `.vite/renderer/main_window` (renderer) are the two source-map roots `sentry-cli upload-sourcemaps --url-prefix "app:///"` needs to target — both bundles end up addressed via the `app:///` prefix by `rewriteFramesIntegration`.

## Desired End State

1. `git tag @lightfast/desktop@0.1.0 && git push origin '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0'` fires `desktop-release.yml`, which builds signed+notarized arm64 and x64 `.zip` + `.dmg`, uploads source maps to Sentry, publishes GitHub release assets with Squirrel.Mac JSON feeds, and undrafts the release.
2. Packaged app's Sentry issues have fully symbolicated stacks (file/line → repo file/line), session tag, `dist`, and the `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0+<runNumber>` release id.
3. Every PR touching `apps/desktop/**` runs typecheck + `electron-forge package` on macOS and fails if either breaks.
4. A new contributor runs `cp apps/desktop/.env.example apps/desktop/.env.development && pnpm dev:desktop` and the app boots.
5. `pnpm changeset` in the repo never prompts for `@lightfast/desktop`.

Verification:
- `gh release view '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0' --repo lightfastai/lightfast` shows 6 assets (arm64 zip+dmg+json, x64 zip+dmg+json) plus undraft state.
- `sentry-cli releases info '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0+<runNumber>' --project lightfast-desktop` lists uploaded source maps.
- `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` exists and is green on the tag push.
- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop lint` passes.

## What We're NOT Doing

- Sparkle-native / Ed25519 auto-update (Squirrel.Mac is fine for v0.1.0). `sparklePublicKey` gets deleted, not stamped.
- Windows + Linux packaging (`MakerSquirrel` stays in `forge.config.ts:82-85` dormant; no Linux makers; no Windows job in the workflow).
- Universal binaries (`@electron/universal`). Two separate `-arm64` / `-x64` artifacts are published; the existing `${arch}` template in `updater.ts:27-29` handles dispatch.
- File-backed structured logger. Sentry + breadcrumbs cover v0.1.0; revisit once a user-issue-debugging workflow demands it.
- SQLite / persistent structured storage, worker tier, per-surface preload isolation, renderer i18n, Playwright harness, notifications — all deferred per original research `Status Tracker`.
- Wiring desktop into the changeset-driven release flow (`release.yml`). Desktop stays tag-only.
- A cross-repo `lightfastai/lightfast-desktop-releases` release host. Monorepo tags only.

## Implementation Approach

Strict phase order because of dependencies:

- **Phase A (setup)** must happen before D can be tested end-to-end. Runs in parallel with B/C/E/F code work.
- **Phase B** must land before D (D stamps fields that B defines).
- **Phase C** adds the script that D calls.
- **Phases E and F** are independent — can land in either order relative to B–D.

---

## Phase A: External Setup Checklist (human)

### Overview

No code. Provision the four external resources the workflow depends on, and commit secrets to GitHub. Skipping any item here will leave `desktop-release.yml` failing on the first run.

### Apple Developer (8 secrets)

1. Enroll in **Apple Developer Program** (~$99/yr) under the Lightfast business entity. Capture the 10-character **Team ID** once enrolled.
2. Generate a **Developer ID Application** certificate in developer.apple.com → Certificates. Download the `.cer`, add to Keychain Access on a Mac, export the matching identity as a password-protected `.p12`.
3. Generate an **App Store Connect API key** with "Developer" role (developer.apple.com → Users and Access → Keys). Download the `.p8` (one-time download). Capture the **Key ID** (10-char) and **Issuer ID** (UUID).
4. In repo settings → Secrets and variables → Actions, add these secrets:
   - `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY` — full identity string, e.g. `Developer ID Application: Lightfast AI Inc (ABC1234567)`
   - `APPLE_TEAM_ID` — 10-char team id (e.g. `ABC1234567`)
   - `APPLE_CERT_BASE64` — output of `base64 < DeveloperID.p12 | pbcopy` (macOS) or `base64 -w0 DeveloperID.p12` (Linux)
   - `APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD` — the p12 password chosen at export
   - `APPLE_API_KEY_ID` — the 10-char key id from step 3
   - `APPLE_API_ISSUER` — the issuer UUID from step 3
   - `APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT` — raw contents of the `.p8` file (including the `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----` header/footer)
   - `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD` — any strong random string (e.g. `openssl rand -base64 24`); used only inside the CI keychain

### Sentry (2 secrets + 1 project)

1. In Sentry, create a new **Electron** project named `lightfast-desktop` under the existing Lightfast org. Capture the DSN.
2. Create a Sentry **organization auth token** with `project:releases` scope (Settings → Developer Settings → Auth Tokens).
3. Add these secrets to the repo:
   - `SENTRY_DSN` — the DSN from step 1 (public, but storing as a secret keeps it out of git)
   - `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` — the auth token from step 2
4. Add these repo variables (Variables tab, not Secrets):
   - `SENTRY_ORG` — Sentry org slug (e.g. `lightfast`)
   - `SENTRY_PROJECT` — `lightfast-desktop`

### Success Criteria

Nothing to automate. Cross off the 11 items above; confirm by visiting `github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/settings/secrets/actions` and seeing all 8 Apple secrets + 2 Sentry secrets + 2 Sentry variables present.

**Stop here for human confirmation that Phase A is complete before starting Phase D validation.**

---

## Phase B: Forge config + build-info cleanup

### Overview

Three tiny edits that unblock Phase D: remove the `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` indirection in `forge.config.ts`, drop the unused `sparklePublicKey` field everywhere, and add `sentryDsn` to the build-info schema so Phase D can stamp it via `npm pkg set`.

### Changes Required

#### 1. `apps/desktop/forge.config.ts`

**File**: `apps/desktop/forge.config.ts` (lines 43-54)
**Changes**: Hardcode the release repo; simplify the `githubPublisher` branch.

```ts
// Before:
const [publishOwner, publishRepo] = (
  process.env.LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO ?? ""
).split("/");

const githubPublisher =
  process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN && publishOwner && publishRepo
    ? new PublisherGithub({
        repository: { owner: publishOwner, name: publishRepo },
        draft: true,
        prerelease: process.env.LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_PRERELEASE === "true",
      })
    : null;

// After:
const githubPublisher = process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN
  ? new PublisherGithub({
      repository: { owner: "lightfastai", name: "lightfast" },
      draft: true,
      prerelease: process.env.LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_PRERELEASE === "true",
    })
  : null;
```

Rationale: monorepo is the release host (see plan Q1 resolution). Leaves `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_PRERELEASE` intact for future `0.1.0-rc.1`-style tags.

#### 2. `apps/desktop/package.json`

**File**: `apps/desktop/package.json` (lines 60-64)
**Changes**: Remove `sparklePublicKey`; add `sentryDsn: ""` placeholder (CI stamps it).

```json
// Before (lines 61-64):
"buildFlavor": "dev",
"buildNumber": "1",
"sparkleFeedUrl": "",
"sparklePublicKey": ""

// After:
"buildFlavor": "dev",
"buildNumber": "1",
"sparkleFeedUrl": "",
"sentryDsn": ""
```

#### 3. `apps/desktop/src/shared/env.ts`

**File**: `apps/desktop/src/shared/env.ts` (lines 6-14)
**Changes**: Drop `sparklePublicKey` from `buildInfoSchema`; add `sentryDsn`.

```ts
export const buildInfoSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string(),
  version: z.string(),
  buildFlavor: buildFlavorSchema,
  buildNumber: z.string(),
  sparkleFeedUrl: z.string(),
  sentryDsn: z.string(),
});
```

#### 4. `apps/desktop/src/main/build-info.ts`

**File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/build-info.ts` (lines 16-24)
**Changes**: Stop reading `sparklePublicKey`; start reading `sentryDsn`.

```ts
const candidate = {
  name: packageJson.name,
  version: packageJson.version,
  buildFlavor: runtime.BUILD_FLAVOR ?? packageJson.buildFlavor,
  buildNumber: packageJson.buildNumber,
  sparkleFeedUrl: runtime.SPARKLE_FEED_URL ?? packageJson.sparkleFeedUrl,
  sentryDsn: runtime.SENTRY_DSN ?? packageJson.sentryDsn,
};
```

Runtime `SENTRY_DSN` continues to override for local/dev use — the pattern already exists for `SPARKLE_FEED_URL`.

#### 5. `apps/desktop/src/shared/ipc.ts`

**File**: `apps/desktop/src/shared/ipc.ts` (line 52)
**Changes**: Drop `sparklePublicKey` from `BuildInfoSnapshot`.

```ts
export interface BuildInfoSnapshot {
  buildFlavor: "dev" | "preview" | "prod";
  buildNumber: string;
  name: string;
  sparkleFeedUrl: string;
  version: string;
}
```

#### 6. `apps/desktop/src/main/sentry.ts`

**File**: `apps/desktop/src/main/sentry.ts` (lines 16-26)
**Changes**: Read DSN from build-info (which already composes env + package.json), not directly from env.

```ts
export function getSentryInitOptions(): SentryInitOptions {
  const build = getBuildInfo();
  const dsn = build.sentryDsn;
  return {
    dsn,
    release: `${build.name}@${build.version}+${build.buildNumber}`,
    environment: build.buildFlavor,
    enabled: Boolean(dsn) && build.buildFlavor !== "dev",
  };
}
```

`getRuntimeEnv` import can stay or be removed depending on whether `SESSION_ID` side uses it (it doesn't — it's `randomUUID` from `node:crypto`). Drop the `getRuntimeEnv` import from `sentry.ts` when you're done.

### Success Criteria

#### Automated Verification

- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` passes.
- [ ] `rg -n "sparklePublicKey" apps/desktop` returns zero hits.
- [ ] `rg -n "LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO" apps/desktop` returns zero hits.

#### Manual Verification

- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop dev` boots; no Sentry init happens (dev flavor, DSN empty) — `rg -n "initialized = true" apps/desktop/src/main/sentry.ts` path is not taken.
- [ ] With `SENTRY_DSN=<test-dsn>` and `BUILD_FLAVOR=preview` in the shell env, `pnpm dev` boots and Sentry.init is called (add a temporary `console.log("sentry enabled")` to verify, then revert).

---

## Phase C: Sentry source-map upload script

### Overview

Add a `scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs` that wraps `@sentry/cli` and uploads both `.vite/build` (main + preload) and `.vite/renderer/main_window` (renderer) with `--url-prefix "app:///"` so uploaded maps align with the `rewriteFramesIntegration` prefix set in `sentry.ts:45`. Add `@sentry/cli` to desktop devDeps so the script can invoke it via `pnpm exec sentry-cli`.

### Changes Required

#### 1. `apps/desktop/package.json`

**File**: `apps/desktop/package.json` (devDependencies, alphabetical insertion)
**Changes**: Add `@sentry/cli` to devDependencies; add a `sourcemaps:upload` script.

```json
"scripts": {
  "dev": "electron-forge start",
  "package": "electron-forge package",
  "make": "electron-forge make",
  "publish": "electron-forge publish",
  "sourcemaps:upload": "node scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs",
  "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json && tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.node.json"
},
```

Add `"@sentry/cli": "^2.39.1"` to `devDependencies` (alphabetical slot between `@sentry/electron` and `@tanstack/query-core`). Then run `pnpm install` from repo root to refresh `pnpm-lock.yaml`.

#### 2. `apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs` (new file)

**File**: `apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs`
**Changes**: New file.

```js
#!/usr/bin/env node
import { execFileSync } from "node:child_process";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { dirname, resolve } from "node:path";
import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";

const here = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
const desktopRoot = resolve(here, "..");
const pkg = JSON.parse(
  readFileSync(resolve(desktopRoot, "package.json"), "utf8")
);

const required = ["SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN", "SENTRY_ORG", "SENTRY_PROJECT"];
for (const name of required) {
  if (!process.env[name]) {
    console.error(`Missing env ${name}`);
    process.exit(1);
  }
}

const release = `${pkg.name}@${pkg.version}+${pkg.buildNumber}`;
const urlPrefix = "app:///";
const buildDir = resolve(desktopRoot, ".vite/build");
const rendererDir = resolve(desktopRoot, ".vite/renderer/main_window");

function sentry(args) {
  execFileSync("pnpm", ["exec", "sentry-cli", ...args], {
    cwd: desktopRoot,
    stdio: "inherit",
    env: process.env,
  });
}

sentry(["releases", "new", release]);
sentry([
  "releases",
  "files",
  release,
  "upload-sourcemaps",
  "--url-prefix",
  urlPrefix,
  "--ext",
  "js",
  "--ext",
  "map",
  buildDir,
]);
sentry([
  "releases",
  "files",
  release,
  "upload-sourcemaps",
  "--url-prefix",
  urlPrefix,
  "--ext",
  "js",
  "--ext",
  "map",
  rendererDir,
]);
sentry(["releases", "finalize", release]);

console.log(`Uploaded sourcemaps for release ${release}`);
```

Why two separate upload calls: `sentry-cli upload-sourcemaps` takes one path. Calling twice is cheaper than trying to symlink or merge the two output trees.

### Success Criteria

#### Automated Verification

- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop exec sentry-cli --version` prints a version.
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` still passes (no TS changes, but sanity).
- [ ] `node apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs` with all three env vars missing exits 1 with a clear message.

#### Manual Verification (requires Phase A Sentry secrets)

- [ ] In a scratch shell: `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop package` to populate `.vite/`, then `SENTRY_DSN=<dsn> SENTRY_ORG=<slug> REDACTED SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN=<token> pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop sourcemaps:upload`.
- [ ] In Sentry UI → Releases → `@lightfast/desktop@0.0.0+1`, confirm source maps are listed under Artifacts and at least one path starts with `app:///`.

**Stop here for manual confirmation before Phase D.**

---

## Phase D: Enable + harden `desktop-release.yml`

### Overview

Rename the workflow off `.disabled`, switch the trigger to `@lightfast/desktop@*` tags, add `cancel-in-progress`, stamp `sentryDsn` at build time, call the Phase C upload script, create a Sentry release, add artifact attestation, and generate release notes from the tag's commit range instead of a static string.

### Changes Required

#### 1. Rename file

```
mv .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml.disabled \
   .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml
```

#### 2. `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` (post-rename edits)

**File**: `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml`
**Changes**: Apply the following edits in the file described by the `.disabled` transcript.

**2a. Trigger + concurrency** (lines 3-9 of the existing file)

```yaml
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - '@lightfast/desktop@*'

concurrency:
  group: desktop-release-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true
```

## Phase E: Desktop CI coverage

### Overview

Make every PR that touches `apps/desktop/**` run typecheck + `electron-forge package` on macOS. Prevents packaging regressions from reaching `main`.

### Changes Required

#### 1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

**File**: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
**Changes**: Add a new job `desktop-package` that runs only on PRs/pushes that touch the desktop app, via `paths-filter`. Depend on nothing; include in `ci-success.needs`.

```yaml
# Add after the quality job:
  desktop:
    name: Desktop package
    runs-on: macos-14
    timeout-minutes: 30
    env:
      TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }}
      TURBO_TEAM: ${{ vars.TURBO_TEAM }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Filter
        uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            desktop:
              - 'apps/desktop/**'
              - 'packages/app-trpc/**'
              - 'pnpm-lock.yaml'

      - name: Setup pnpm
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js 22
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Typecheck desktop
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        run: pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck

      - name: Package desktop (unsigned)
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        run: pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop package
```

Update the `ci-success` job's `needs:` array from `[quality]` to `[quality, desktop]`.

Why macos-14 + unsigned package: signing requires the Apple secrets which we don't expose to PRs (untrusted code). `electron-forge package` without sign/notarize still catches 95% of build-time regressions (native module rebuild, forge config, vite build).

`packages/app-trpc/**` is included because desktop imports it (`@repo/app-trpc` workspace dep); changes there can break the desktop build.

#### 2. `.github/workflows/ci-core.yml`

**File**: `.github/workflows/ci-core.yml` (lines 45, 105)
**Changes**: Leave `ci-core.yml` as-is. Its scope is "things we publish to npm" (`lightfast`, `mcp`, `cli`). Desktop is covered by the new `desktop` job in `ci.yml`. No changes.

This is a deliberate boundary: `ci-core.yml` is the npm-publishable slice, `ci.yml` is everything else.

### Success Criteria

#### Automated Verification

- [ ] A PR touching only `apps/www/**` skips the desktop job (paths-filter `desktop` output is `false`).
- [ ] The `desktop` job completes in < 20 minutes on `macos-14`.

#### Manual Verification

- [ ] Open a throwaway PR with a one-char change to `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts`. Confirm both `CI Success` and `Desktop package` show as checks and pass.

## Testing Strategy

### Typecheck + lint gate

Every phase ends with `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` green. Phase F adds `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop lint` to the gate.

### End-to-end release dry run

After Phase D lands and Phase A secrets are provisioned:

1. Tag `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0-rc.1`.
2. Watch the workflow run green end-to-end.
3. Install the arm64 `.dmg` on a personal Mac, sign in, exercise primary + secondary + hud windows.
4. Trigger a known error (temporary `throw` in `src/main/index.ts` on a dev tag), confirm Sentry stack trace is symbolicated.
5. Publish a second release `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0-rc.2` with a no-op change; confirm auto-updater picks it up on the running `rc.1` install within 10 seconds of launch.
6. Revert the `throw` and delete the pre-release tags.

### CI gate regression

Open a throwaway PR with a forced break in `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts` (e.g. `const x: number = "string"`). Confirm the new `desktop` job fails loudly and blocks merge. Revert before merging.

## Performance Considerations

- `desktop` job in `ci.yml` adds ~15 min to PR CI time on desktop-touching PRs (macos-14 runner + install + package). Acceptable; the alternative is packaging regressions in `main`.
- Source-map upload adds ~30s to each release arch leg. Acceptable.
- Sentry release creation is a single API call, negligible.

## Migration Notes

- Pre-release installs of the unsigned dev build (`pnpm package` output) won't auto-update. Not a concern — nobody is running the dev `.app` outside local testing.
- `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` env var is gone after Phase B. If anyone has it in a local `.env.development`, remove it. No on-disk state affected.

# Desktop Pre-Release Batch Implementation Plan

## Overview

Get `apps/desktop` from "quick-wins applied" to "first public `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0` release shipping a signed, notarized, auto-updating macOS binary with Sentry-readable stack traces." Six phases. Phase A is a human-setup checklist; B–F are code/CI changes. All four prior quick-win phases are already applied and verified in the current tree.

## Current State Analysis

All four phases of `2026-04-23-desktop-codex-gap-quick-wins.md` are landed:
- Entitlements trimmed (`build/entitlements.mac.plist` has 5 keys; over-broad `disable-library-validation`/`device.camera`/`network.server` removed).
- `forge.config.ts:67-78` has `NSQuitAlwaysKeepsWindows=false`, `LSMinimumSystemVersion=12.0`, `LSEnvironment.MallocNanoZone="0"`.
- `src/main/sentry.ts:14-55` has `SESSION_ID`, `rewriteFramesIntegration({root, prefix: "app:///"})`, `dist=buildNumber`, `bundle/host/sessionId` tags.
- `showContextMenu` IPC, `silentRefresh`, and the deep-link `console.log` are gone. `protocol.ts` deleted; OAuth moved to a loopback HTTP server (`auth-flow.ts:70-141`).

What still blocks a v0.1.0 release:
- `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml.disabled` is inert (correctly, since Apple signing secrets aren't provisioned yet).
- `forge.config.ts:43-54` reads `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` but the disabled workflow passes `${{ github.repository }}` — two conflicting paths.
- `@sentry/cli` is in `pnpm-workspace.yaml:82` `onlyBuiltDependencies` but no script invokes it; packaged-build stack traces will be unreadable despite `rewriteFramesIntegration` being wired.
- `ci-core.yml:45,105` explicitly excludes desktop from typecheck + build. `ci.yml:50-54` only runs typecheck via `turbo --affected`. Nothing runs `electron-forge package` on PRs.
- `apps/desktop/package.json` placeholders: `version: "0.0.0"`, empty `sparkleFeedUrl`, unused `sparklePublicKey: ""`.
- No `.env.example`, no `lint`/`clean` scripts in desktop, `dev:desktop-stack` is misnamed at the root.

### Key Discoveries

- `sparklePublicKey` is declared in `src/shared/env.ts:12`, `src/shared/ipc.ts:52`, and `src/main/build-info.ts:23` but has **zero** downstream readers (grep confirms). Electron's `autoUpdater` does not use it. Drop the field instead of stamping it in CI.
- Sentry DSNs are public identifiers (they're visible in any shipped app's JS bundle). Baking at CI build time via `npm pkg set sentryDsn=...` from a secret is simplest — no runtime env plumbing required on macOS.
- Release tag convention in this repo is already `<package>@<version>` (changesets produces `lightfast@x.y.z`, `@lightfastai/mcp@x.y.z` via `release.yml`). Desktop should match: `@lightfast/desktop@<version>`. YAML trigger: `'@lightfast/desktop@*'`.
- Desktop is `"private": true`; `changesets/action` ignores private packages automatically — it cannot and will not try to publish desktop. The blocker isn't publishing, it's that `verify-changeset.yml:51` rejects any changeset file mentioning `@lightfast/desktop`. We stay tag-only and add the package to `.changeset/config.json` `ignore` so `pnpm changeset` never prompts for it.
- Monorepo is public (`gh repo view` → `"visibility":"PUBLIC"`). Sparkle feed URL `github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/releases/latest/download/latest-mac-${arch}.json` works without auth.
- `.vite/build` (main + preload) and `.vite/renderer/main_window` (renderer) are the two source-map roots `sentry-cli upload-sourcemaps --url-prefix "app:///"` needs to target — both bundles end up addressed via the `app:///` prefix by `rewriteFramesIntegration`.

## Desired End State

1. `git tag @lightfast/desktop@0.1.0 && git push origin '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0'` fires `desktop-release.yml`, which builds signed+notarized arm64 and x64 `.zip` + `.dmg`, uploads source maps to Sentry, publishes GitHub release assets with Squirrel.Mac JSON feeds, and undrafts the release.
2. Packaged app's Sentry issues have fully symbolicated stacks (file/line → repo file/line), session tag, `dist`, and the `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0+<runNumber>` release id.
3. Every PR touching `apps/desktop/**` runs typecheck + `electron-forge package` on macOS and fails if either breaks.
4. A new contributor runs `cp apps/desktop/.env.example apps/desktop/.env.development && pnpm dev:desktop` and the app boots.
5. `pnpm changeset` in the repo never prompts for `@lightfast/desktop`.

Verification:
- `gh release view '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0' --repo lightfastai/lightfast` shows 6 assets (arm64 zip+dmg+json, x64 zip+dmg+json) plus undraft state.
- `sentry-cli releases info '@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0+<runNumber>' --project lightfast-desktop` lists uploaded source maps.
- `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` exists and is green on the tag push.
- `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop lint` passes.

## What We're NOT Doing

- Sparkle-native / Ed25519 auto-update (Squirrel.Mac is fine for v0.1.0). `sparklePublicKey` gets deleted, not stamped.
- Windows + Linux packaging (`MakerSquirrel` stays in `forge.config.ts:82-85` dormant; no Linux makers; no Windows job in the workflow).
- Universal binaries (`@electron/universal`). Two separate `-arm64` / `-x64` artifacts are published; the existing `${arch}` template in `updater.ts:27-29` handles dispatch.
- File-backed structured logger. Sentry + breadcrumbs cover v0.1.0; revisit once a user-issue-debugging workflow demands it.
- SQLite / persistent structured storage, worker tier, per-surface preload isolation, renderer i18n, Playwright harness, notifications — all deferred per original research `Status Tracker`.
- Wiring desktop into the changeset-driven release flow (`release.yml`). Desktop stays tag-only.
- A cross-repo `lightfastai/lightfast-desktop-releases` release host. Monorepo tags only.

## Implementation Approach

Strict phase order because of dependencies:

- **Phase A (setup)** must happen before D can be tested end-to-end. Runs in parallel with B/C/E/F code work.
- **Phase B** must land before D (D stamps fields that B defines).
- **Phase C** adds the script that D calls.
- **Phases E and F** are independent — can land in either order relative to B–D.

---

## Phase A: External Setup Checklist (human)

### Overview

No code. Provision the four external resources the workflow depends on, and commit secrets to GitHub. Skipping any item here will leave `desktop-release.yml` failing on the first run.

### Apple Developer (8 secrets)

1. Enroll in **Apple Developer Program** (~$99/yr) under the Lightfast business entity. Capture the 10-character **Team ID** once enrolled.
2. Generate a **Developer ID Application** certificate in developer.apple.com → Certificates. Download the `.cer`, add to Keychain Access on a Mac, export the matching identity as a password-protected `.p12`.
3. Generate an **App Store Connect API key** with "Developer" role (developer.apple.com → Users and Access → Keys). Download the `.p8` (one-time download). Capture the **Key ID** (10-char) and **Issuer ID** (UUID).
4. In repo settings → Secrets and variables → Actions, add these secrets:
   - `APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY` — full identity string, e.g. `Developer ID Application: Lightfast AI Inc (ABC1234567)`
   - `APPLE_TEAM_ID` — 10-char team id (e.g. `ABC1234567`)
   - `APPLE_CERT_BASE64` — output of `base64 < DeveloperID.p12 | pbcopy` (macOS) or `base64 -w0 DeveloperID.p12` (Linux)
   - `APPLE_CERT_PASSWORD` — the p12 password chosen at export
   - `APPLE_API_KEY_ID` — the 10-char key id from step 3
   - `APPLE_API_ISSUER` — the issuer UUID from step 3
   - `APPLE_API_KEY_CONTENT` — raw contents of the `.p8` file (including the `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----` header/footer)
   - `KEYCHAIN_PASSWORD` — any strong random string (e.g. `openssl rand -base64 24`); used only inside the CI keychain

### Sentry (2 secrets + 1 project)

1. In Sentry, create a new **Electron** project named `lightfast-desktop` under the existing Lightfast org. Capture the DSN.
2. Create a Sentry **organization auth token** with `project:releases` scope (Settings → Developer Settings → Auth Tokens).
3. Add these secrets to the repo:
   - `SENTRY_DSN` — the DSN from step 1 (public, but storing as a secret keeps it out of git)
   - `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` — the auth token from step 2
4. Add these repo variables (Variables tab, not Secrets):
   - `SENTRY_ORG` — Sentry org slug (e.g. `lightfast`)
   - `SENTRY_PROJECT` — `lightfast-desktop`

### Success Criteria

Nothing to automate. Cross off the 11 items above; confirm by visiting `github.com/lightfastai/lightfast/settings/secrets/actions` and seeing all 8 Apple secrets + 2 Sentry secrets + 2 Sentry variables present.

**Stop here for human confirmation that Phase A is complete before starting Phase D validation.**

---

## Phase B: Forge config + build-info cleanup

### Overview

## Phase C: Sentry source-map upload script

### Overview

Add a `scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs` that wraps `@sentry/cli` and uploads both `.vite/build` (main + preload) and `.vite/renderer/main_window` (renderer) with `--url-prefix "app:///"` so uploaded maps align with the `rewriteFramesIntegration` prefix set in `sentry.ts:45`. Add `@sentry/cli` to desktop devDeps so the script can invoke it via `pnpm exec sentry-cli`.

### Changes Required

#### 1. `apps/desktop/package.json`

**File**: `apps/desktop/package.json` (devDependencies, alphabetical insertion)
**Changes**: Add `@sentry/cli` to devDependencies; add a `sourcemaps:upload` script.

```json
"scripts": {
  "dev": "electron-forge start",
  "package": "electron-forge package",
  "make": "electron-forge make",
  "publish": "electron-forge publish",
  "sourcemaps:upload": "node scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs",
  "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json && tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.node.json"
},
```

Add `"@sentry/cli": "^2.39.1"` to `devDependencies` (alphabetical slot between `@sentry/electron` and `@tanstack/query-core`). Then run `pnpm install` from repo root to refresh `pnpm-lock.yaml`.

#### 2. `apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs` (new file)

**File**: `apps/desktop/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.mjs`
**Changes**: New file.

```js
#!/usr/bin/env node
import { execFileSync } from "node:child_process";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { dirname, resolve } from "node:path";
import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";

const here = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
const desktopRoot = resolve(here, "..");
const pkg = JSON.parse(
  readFileSync(resolve(desktopRoot, "package.json"), "utf8")
);

const required = ["SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN", "SENTRY_ORG", "SENTRY_PROJECT"];
for (const name of required) {
  if (!process.env[name]) {
    console.error(`Missing env ${name}`);
    process.exit(1);
  }
}

const release = `${pkg.name}@${pkg.version}+${pkg.buildNumber}`;
const urlPrefix = "app:///";
const buildDir = resolve(desktopRoot, ".vite/build");
const rendererDir = resolve(desktopRoot, ".vite/renderer/main_window");

function sentry(args) {
  execFileSync("pnpm", ["exec", "sentry-cli", ...args], {
    cwd: desktopRoot,
    stdio: "inherit",
    env: process.env,
  });
}

sentry(["releases", "new", release]);
sentry([
  "releases",
  "files",
  release,
  "upload-sourcemaps",
  "--url-prefix",
  urlPrefix,
  "--ext",
  "js",
  "--ext",
  "map",
  buildDir,
]);
sentry([
  "releases",
  "files",
  release,
  "upload-sourcemaps",
  "--url-prefix",
  urlPrefix,
  "--ext",
  "js",
  "--ext",
  "map",
  rendererDir,
]);
sentry(["releases", "finalize", release]);

console.log(`Uploaded sourcemaps for release ${release}`);
```

Why two separate upload calls: `sentry-cli upload-sourcemaps` takes one path. Calling twice is cheaper than trying to symlink or merge the two output trees.

## Phase E: Desktop CI coverage

### Overview

Make every PR that touches `apps/desktop/**` run typecheck + `electron-forge package` on macOS. Prevents packaging regressions from reaching `main`.

### Changes Required

#### 1. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

**File**: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
**Changes**: Add a new job `desktop-package` that runs only on PRs/pushes that touch the desktop app, via `paths-filter`. Depend on nothing; include in `ci-success.needs`.

```yaml
# Add after the quality job:
  desktop:
    name: Desktop package
    runs-on: macos-14
    timeout-minutes: 30
    env:
      TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }}
      TURBO_TEAM: ${{ vars.TURBO_TEAM }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Filter
        uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            desktop:
              - 'apps/desktop/**'
              - 'packages/app-trpc/**'
              - 'pnpm-lock.yaml'

      - name: Setup pnpm
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js 22
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: 'pnpm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Typecheck desktop
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        run: pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck

      - name: Package desktop (unsigned)
        if: steps.filter.outputs.desktop == 'true'
        run: pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop package
```

Update the `ci-success` job's `needs:` array from `[quality]` to `[quality, desktop]`.

Why macos-14 + unsigned package: signing requires the Apple secrets which we don't expose to PRs (untrusted code). `electron-forge package` without sign/notarize still catches 95% of build-time regressions (native module rebuild, forge config, vite build).

`packages/app-trpc/**` is included because desktop imports it (`@repo/app-trpc` workspace dep); changes there can break the desktop build.

#### 2. `.github/workflows/ci-core.yml`

**File**: `.github/workflows/ci-core.yml` (lines 45, 105)
**Changes**: Leave `ci-core.yml` as-is. Its scope is "things we publish to npm" (`lightfast`, `mcp`, `cli`). Desktop is covered by the new `desktop` job in `ci.yml`. No changes.

This is a deliberate boundary: `ci-core.yml` is the npm-publishable slice, `ci.yml` is everything else.

### Success Criteria

#### Automated Verification

- [ ] A PR touching only `apps/desktop/src/main/sentry.ts` triggers the new `desktop` job and skips its own work otherwise (confirm via the paths-filter `if:` guards).
- [ ] A PR touching only `apps/www/**` skips the desktop job (paths-filter `desktop` output is `false`).
- [ ] The `desktop` job completes in < 20 minutes on `macos-14`.

- [ ] Open a throwaway PR with a one-char change to `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts`. Confirm both `CI Success` and `Desktop package` show as checks and pass.

## Testing Strategy

### Typecheck + lint gate

Every phase ends with `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop typecheck` green. Phase F adds `pnpm --filter @lightfast/desktop lint` to the gate.

### End-to-end release dry run

After Phase D lands and Phase A secrets are provisioned:

1. Tag `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0-rc.1`.
2. Watch the workflow run green end-to-end.
3. Install the arm64 `.dmg` on a personal Mac, sign in, exercise primary + secondary + hud windows.
4. Trigger a known error (temporary `throw` in `src/main/index.ts` on a dev tag), confirm Sentry stack trace is symbolicated.
5. Publish a second release `@lightfast/desktop@0.1.0-rc.2` with a no-op change; confirm auto-updater picks it up on the running `rc.1` install within 10 seconds of launch.
6. Revert the `throw` and delete the pre-release tags.

### CI gate regression

Open a throwaway PR with a forced break in `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts` (e.g. `const x: number = "string"`). Confirm the new `desktop` job fails loudly and blocks merge. Revert before merging.

## Performance Considerations

- `desktop` job in `ci.yml` adds ~15 min to PR CI time on desktop-touching PRs (macos-14 runner + install + package). Acceptable; the alternative is packaging regressions in `main`.
- Source-map upload adds ~30s to each release arch leg. Acceptable.
- Sentry release creation is a single API call, negligible.

## Migration Notes

- Pre-release installs of the unsigned dev build (`pnpm package` output) won't auto-update. Not a concern — nobody is running the dev `.app` outside local testing.
- `LIGHTFAST_DESKTOP_RELEASE_REPO` env var is gone after Phase B. If anyone has it in a local `.env.development`, remove it. No on-disk state affected.
